content:
@Rick Gleason reports:

I've tried to update-templates VDR_pi twice and all I get is a bunch of
these.

What is the problem?

https://github.com/OpenCPN/plugins/runs/5859820856?check_suite_focus=true

The link contains 

The value 'ubuntu-wx315-x86_64' is not an element of the set {'all', 'android-arm64-v8a', 'android-armeabi-v7a',
'android-armhf', 'android-arm64', 'darwin', 'darwin-wx315', 'debian-x86_64', 'debian-armhf', 'flatpak-aarch64',
'flatpak-x86_64', 'flatpak-x86_64-wx315', 'mingw', 'mingw-x86_64', 'msvc', 'ubuntu-gtk3-x86_64', 'ubuntu-x86_64',
'ubuntu-gtk3-armhf', 'ubuntu-armhf', 'raspbian-armhf'}.

That is, it builds a (yet) not defined ABI ubuntu-wx315-x86_64

This is https://github.com/Rasbats/shipdriver_pi/issues/437, filed by you and fixed in 3.1.1. So you could just update to 3.1.1 to fix problem, or remove the offending build manually

Thanks Alec, I think was confused and linked to the first version using outdated update-template sd3.0.1-beta (VDR 1.3.22). Recently I updated-template sd3.1.1 (VDR 1.3.23) which all built 14 hours ago That built and is all available in Cloudsmith Now isn't something done automatically. Then I have to complete it somehow? PS Sorry for the pesky questions. I have to get the routine remembered. I want to do same with logbook too.
I now see my rgleason/plugins/auto Shows both the VDR 1.3.22 and 1.3.23

Now what? It turns into a big mess after this.

fcgle@Hesper MINGW64 ~/source/plugins (master)
$ git remote update upstream
Fetching upstream

fcgle@Hesper MINGW64 ~/source/plugins (master)
$ git checkout master
Already on 'master'
Your branch is up to date with 'origin/master'.

$ git fetch origin auto:auto
Enter passphrase for key '/c/Users/fcgle/.ssh/id_ed25519':
Enter passphrase for key '/c/Users/fcgle/.ssh/id_ed25519':

fcgle@Hesper MINGW64 ~/source/plugins (master)
$ git checkout auto
Switched to branch 'auto'
Your branch and 'upstream/master' have diverged,
and have 50 and 34 different commits each, respectively.
(use "git pull" to merge the remote branch into yours)
BTW I have Logbook v1.4.29 here in circleci and I am finding v1.4.28 and 1.4.29 in my plugins/auto

I have half a mind to just copy the right stuff, change to Master branch and paste it in.
That is a lot simpler.

If you are really sure that you a) have the correct contents in your local master branch and b) that noone is using your repo besides you you can force-push using git push -f origin master:master. This will effectively overwrite the history in your remote repo, and break the overall git promise that nothing ever is lost. But it will work

If you have the correct content in the remote branch you can do like this:

$ git checkout -b tmp
$ git remote update origin
$ git branch -m master master.old
$ git fetch origin master:master
$ git checkout master

This will give a master branch which is a copy of the remote master branch, saving you local master branch as master.old. Here, everything is still available, but you need to be careful when re-constructing master.

Don't copy files. It's generally a bad idea, and even more so on windows where you more or less always get permissions problems

Thanks Alec . What branch am I supposed to execute the above from?
I absolutely HATE that goddamn Bash Editor which is required when merge conflicts occur, and they always occur at the worst time. It is a totally impossible interface. I've tried to read the instructions and it still makes no sense and does not work as expected.

PS: Why should I care about my plugins history?
Let's start over. My remote rgleason/plugins "auto" branch has the metadata for vdr 1.3.23 and logbookkonni 1.4.29 done yesterday. How do I get these into the master catalog?
Please keep this simple.

From stackoverflow
press "i" (i for insert)
write your merge message
press "esc" (escape)
write ":wq" (write & quit)
then press enter
This does work, but very strange.

I have no idea how I got here, but the changes for VDR 1.3.23 are now pushed to remote and I tried to make a PR
and it still fails!!!! This is totally ridiculous. I have updated to sd3.1.1!!!!!!!!
https://github.com/OpenCPN/plugins/pull/649

I would have had this Done several hours ago using TP

if you have metadata in the auto branch, the procedure is in https://opencpn-manuals.github.io/main/AlternativeWorkflow/InstallConfigure/Catalog-Github-Integration.html#make-PR

That procedure rebases your auto branch. This means that the merge operation is fast-forward, and you should not have to enter any commit message. The key bindings from stackoverflow is from using vim which is my everyday editor, so it's not strange for me. But basically, if you come into such a window you are doing something wrong. In particular, you havn't rebased hence getting a messy merge.

Incidentally,
I note that what I have done is so SCREWED up. Commits that I thought were for 1.3.23 and labeled accordingly are actually overwriting 1.3.23 with 1.3.22! Etc.
With this system everything seems to be upside down again. I will try this again since I have no choice.
One useful command is git reset --hard <commit>. It basically throws away anything after the given commit, moving your branch back int time to a hopefully sane starting point

For "Synching Branches" what branches are we talking about here?
Where do I start? What branch?
ARe you making a PR against master or Beta?
I intend to make a PR against Opencpn/plugins/master
Well, as described in that chapter you can then just do the three first commands which updates master

So execute these from my local rgleason/plugins master branch?
git remote update upstream
git checkout master
git rebase upstream/master
from any branch, actually
Totally independent of what branch? I never understood that.
The first is about the remote i. e., all branches. The second will move you to master branch...

OK, have done it. There are problems. I guess I will just
"One useful command is git reset --hard <commit>"

Yes, you have probably some messy commits. Use git reset --hard to go to a sane point in your history. git log --oneline -20or so displays the history, so you can select which commit you wan to come back to.

If you can see upstream/masterin your history a reasonable approach might be git reset --hard upstream/master. However, be aware that anything after that commit is lost.

OK, that master branch is seriously broken. You need to reset it. Do:

$ git checkout -b foo
$ git remote update upstream
$ git fetch -f upstream master:master
$ git checkout master

This will give you a fresh, rebased version of the master branch.
You can remove the foo branch when done: git branch -D foo

Alec, looking at this a little further. The update-template sd3.1.1 run resulting in VDR 1.3.23 and for some reason that is not validating "metadata/VDR-v1.3.23-ubuntu-wx315-20.04.xml fails to validate"

Now I see this in Shipdriver

@Rasbats
Alec Leamas and Rasbats .circleci/config: Remove ubuntu-wx315 build (#437).
https://github.com/Rasbats/shipdriver_pi/commit/5152ee1cff546742191f7223b867224a355284c4

What's going on here? I used the update-templates sd3.1.1 and that should be current. Now the entire push is canceled every time, when it is just a problem not created by me or the plugin.

First: If you have updated to 3.1.1 there is no ubuntu-wx315 build. This is the fix for #437 which you filed. Assuming this is about VDR, please push your master branch to tmp using 'git push origin master:tmp`so I can have a look.

Second: I have rebased your auto branch. Don't know what has happened to it, perhaps you merged something into it, dunno. To get the rebased auto branch from me to your plugins repo:

$ cd plugins
$ git remote add leamas  https://github.com/leamas/plugins.git
$ git remote update leamas
$ git branch -m auto auto.old
$ git fetch leamas rgleason/auto:auto
$ git checkout auto

This will place you in position to merge the auto branch into master as described in the manual. Note that you should not fetch the auto branch as described there (first command), you have already fetched from me instead.

Alec, Thank you. I have pushed vdr_pi to origin master:tmp
and will do the plugins git commands and report back.

Master Catalog
Adding metadata files from the OpenCPN/plugins fork auto branch to the master branch goes like this:
git fetch origin auto:auto <--- Already did this from Leamas repos
git checkout auto <--- already done above.
git rebase master
git checkout master
git merge --squash auto
git commit

with '#' will be ignored, and an empty message aborts the commit.

Of course, so to speak. All builds you do pushes to the same auto branch. Thus, what you merge is all successful builds since you reset your auto branch. The branch is created in Github OpenCPN/plugins fork. Each time you do this, all builds which are present in auto but yet not made to PR will be lost. For that reason, you should avoid these steps after being done, it's just an initial setup. The only exception is when changing from master to Beta or the other way around

you miss the build step in between. This is how it works:

- You make a local change in vdr_pi, removing .drone.yml
- You push vdr_pi to github
- github triggers the builds. Each build pushes metadata changes to auto in the very end
- When all builds are completed you can merge auto into master in plugins repo and make a PR
OK I really have to start the whole process over again, including the builds? Don't I need to increment the version again and add a new tag in order to get it to go into Prod?
Also I didn't have .drone.yml in my local, so I removed it in the remote.
Yes. The build should be tagged to go into prod or stable. These kind of problems is why I prefer to first run the builds untagged, check for problems, tag, push tags and rebuild.
Ok thanks. So I have to do the process all over again, no shortcuts because the commits initiate all the subsequent actions. This is pretty crucial to understand I think.
Yes, do the process again. lesson learned: Don't try to merge auto until the builds are OK

Basic message: do what's described in the docs. No more, and no less.

If not, file an an issue against the manual. The game here is really to read, understand and possibly fix the manual so it describes the workflow. There should not really be any personal annotations or anything like that.

So the SD system is very automatic once you have committed, and any mistake or error requires a new version and eventually a tag to get it into PROD. Then the second step is to get the metadata pushed to a catalog.

Yes. However, there is no need for a new tag (i. e., a new version) if you first run your build without any tag. What we do in shipdriver, a typical example:

  • When we start working the in the 3.1.x branch we set the version to 3.1.0. This is just so that the artifacts will have a 3.1 in the filename.
  • We commit various changes and builds. No tags. Checks that the build looks ok.
  • After a while we need to release a version. The final finish is usually just to update the Changelog file, which contains the release date.
  • This last commit is tagged and built. We now know that the build will be OK, things runs smoothly.

Ok, that approach to versioning makes sense for SD where there is incremental work to get things right.
Often if we are just updating a PI's SD frontend there are fewer steps invovled.

Yes, that's right. Good idea. It was not building because I needed a commit. Alternatively

git tag v1.2.3
git push v1.2.3
git commit --allow-empty -m "Trigger build"
git push origin

OR do it all at the same time

git tag v1.2.3
git commit -am "1.2.3 release to master branch"
git push origin --atomic refs/tags/v1.2.3:refs/tags/v1.2.3 master:master

  • reset the auto branch according to https://opencpn-manuals.github.io/main/AlternativeWorkflow/InstallConfigure/GithubPreps.html, step 2 "Creating the auto branch"
